---
title: "MN26 Pipeline Guide"
subtitle: "Code review documentation for the Minnesota 2026 ETL pipeline"
author: "Kidsights Data Platform"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-expand: 2
    number-sections: true
    number-depth: 3
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-line-numbers: false
    code-copy: true
    theme:
      light: cosmo
    highlight-style: github
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    tbl-cap-location: top
    callout-appearance: simple
    callout-icon: true
  gfm:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
execute:
  eval: false
  echo: true
---

# Quick Start {#sec-quick-start}

::: {.callout-tip}
**Reading this in raw `.qmd` form?** This document is designed to be
rendered to HTML for the best experience (sidebar TOC, diagram, syntax
highlighting). To render:

```bash
quarto render docs/mn26/pipeline_guide.qmd --to html
# then open docs/mn26/pipeline_guide.html in a browser
```

The rendered HTML is gitignored per repository convention — each reader
renders their own copy.
:::

::: {.callout-note}
This document is the **authoritative reference** for the MN26 (Minnesota 2026)
pipeline. It targets a code reviewer who has not worked on the project before
and who needs to (a) run the pipeline against production REDCap data and
(b) audit how raw REDCap fields get recoded into derived variables.

For a deep statistical review of the GRM scoring math, see the
[KidsightsPublic package](https://github.com/marcus-waldman/KidsightsPublic).
The MN26 pipeline treats scoring as a black-box function and validates only
that inputs and outputs are well-formed.
:::

## TL;DR: Run the pipeline in one line

**Prerequisite:** A CSV of REDCap API credentials at
`C:/my_auths/kidsights_redcap_norc_MN_2026.csv` (format: `project,pid,api_code`).

**Command (from project root):**

```bash
"C:/Program Files/R/R-4.5.1/bin/Rscript.exe" run_mn26_pipeline.R --skip-database
```

The `--skip-database` flag runs the entire pipeline **except** the three
DuckDB insertion steps. This is the recommended mode for a first run: it
proves extraction, pivot, transforms, eligibility, and scoring all work
end-to-end without modifying any persistent state. Drop the flag to store
results in `data/duckdb/kidsights_local.duckdb`.

## Expected output

As of the most recent production run (4 NORC REDCap projects,
10,200 records), you should see this summary at the end:

```
========================================
MN26 Pipeline Complete
========================================
  Records extracted:    10200
  Records after pivot:  10517 (child 1: 10200, child 2: 317)
  Eligible:             964
  Meets inclusion:      964
  Kidsights scored:     848
  Total duration:       2.0 seconds
========================================
```

Key numbers to sanity-check:

- **Records after pivot > records extracted.** The pipeline pivots wide
  (1 row per household) to long (1 row per child). Households with two
  children contribute two rows. See [pivot logic](#sec-overview).
- **Eligible « extracted.** NORC projects contain records at all screening
  stages, not just consented participants. Only records with `eq001 == 1`
  (informed consent) AND `age_in_days_n <= 1825` (child 0–5 years) pass
  eligibility. See [eligibility logic](#sec-eligibility) for details.
- **Scored < eligible.** `KidsightsPublic::score_kidsights()` requires at
  least 5 non-missing developmental item responses. Eligible children who
  fell short of this threshold (e.g., partial completions) get `NA` for
  `kidsights_theta`.

::: {.callout-tip}
If your run produces very different numbers, that's usually a sign that
REDCap data has changed (new records, schema updates) rather than a
pipeline bug. Compare your numbers to the commit log of the
`kidsights_redcap_norc_MN_2026.csv` credentials file to see if new projects
were added.
:::

## What to do next

1. Skim the [Pipeline Overview](#sec-overview) diagram to see the full flow.
2. Use the [Code Organization Map](#sec-code-map) to find the file
   responsible for each concern.
3. For a code review focused on recoding accuracy, jump to
   [Variable Recoding Reference](#sec-recoding).
4. If something fails, see [Troubleshooting](#sec-troubleshooting).

# Pipeline Overview {#sec-overview}

## Data flow diagram

The diagram below shows the 10-step MN26 pipeline with **real row counts
from the most recent production run** (10,200 records across 4 NORC
REDCap projects). Steps are color-coded by phase and annotated with the
file responsible for each step.

```{=html}
<style>
  .mn26-pipeline-diagram {
    max-width: 960px;
    margin: 1.5rem auto;
    font-family: -apple-system, "Segoe UI", Roboto, sans-serif;
  }
  .mn26-pipeline-diagram svg {
    width: 100%;
    height: auto;
  }
  .mn26-legend {
    display: flex;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
    gap: 1rem;
    justify-content: center;
    margin-top: 1rem;
    font-size: 0.85rem;
  }
  .mn26-legend span {
    display: inline-flex;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 0.4rem;
  }
  .mn26-legend .swatch {
    width: 14px;
    height: 14px;
    border-radius: 3px;
    display: inline-block;
  }
</style>

<div class="mn26-pipeline-diagram">
<svg viewBox="0 0 960 1280" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="MN26 pipeline data flow diagram">
  <defs>
    <marker id="mn26arrow" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto-start-reverse">
      <path d="M 0 0 L 10 5 L 0 10 z" fill="#475569"/>
    </marker>
    <style>
      .step-box { rx: 8; ry: 8; stroke-width: 1.5; }
      .step-title { font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; fill: #0f172a; }
      .step-file  { font-size: 11px; font-family: Consolas, "Courier New", monospace; fill: #475569; }
      .phase-extract  { fill: #dbeafe; stroke: #2563eb; }
      .phase-pivot    { fill: #e0f2fe; stroke: #0284c7; }
      .phase-store    { fill: #f1f5f9; stroke: #64748b; }
      .phase-transform{ fill: #dcfce7; stroke: #16a34a; }
      .phase-eligible { fill: #fef3c7; stroke: #d97706; }
      .phase-score    { fill: #fae8ff; stroke: #c026d3; }
      .arrow-label    { font-size: 12px; fill: #1e293b; font-weight: 500; }
      .count-badge    { font-size: 13px; font-weight: 700; fill: #b91c1c; }
      .flow-line      { stroke: #475569; stroke-width: 2; fill: none; }
      .external       { fill: #fff; stroke: #94a3b8; stroke-dasharray: 5 3; }
      .external-text  { font-size: 13px; fill: #475569; font-style: italic; }
    </style>
  </defs>

  <!-- Title -->
  <text x="480" y="30" text-anchor="middle" font-size="18" font-weight="700" fill="#0f172a">MN26 Pipeline — 10 Steps</text>
  <text x="480" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#64748b">Data flow with production row counts (4 NORC REDCap projects)</text>

  <!-- External: REDCap source (outside pipeline) -->
  <rect x="330" y="80" width="300" height="50" class="external"/>
  <text x="480" y="102" text-anchor="middle" class="external-text">REDCap API (UNMC server)</text>
  <text x="480" y="120" text-anchor="middle" class="step-file">4 projects: Kidsights Survey NORC 1–4</text>

  <!-- Step 1: Credentials -->
  <rect x="80" y="160" width="380" height="60" class="step-box phase-extract"/>
  <text x="270" y="185" text-anchor="middle" class="step-title">Step 1 — Load API credentials</text>
  <text x="270" y="205" text-anchor="middle" class="step-file">R/extract/mn26.R :: load_mn26_credentials()</text>

  <!-- Step 2: Extract -->
  <rect x="500" y="160" width="380" height="60" class="step-box phase-extract"/>
  <text x="690" y="185" text-anchor="middle" class="step-title">Step 2 — Extract data + dictionary</text>
  <text x="690" y="205" text-anchor="middle" class="step-file">R/extract/mn26.R :: extract_mn26_data()</text>

  <!-- Arrow from REDCap to Step 2 -->
  <path d="M 630 130 L 690 160" class="flow-line" marker-end="url(#mn26arrow)"/>
  <!-- Step 1 → Step 2 -->
  <path d="M 460 190 L 500 190" class="flow-line" marker-end="url(#mn26arrow)"/>

  <!-- Count badge after Step 2 -->
  <path d="M 690 220 L 690 270" class="flow-line" marker-end="url(#mn26arrow)"/>
  <rect x="600" y="232" width="180" height="22" rx="4" ry="4" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#fecaca"/>
  <text x="690" y="248" text-anchor="middle" class="count-badge">10,200 records (wide)</text>

  <!-- Step 3: Pivot -->
  <rect x="500" y="270" width="380" height="60" class="step-box phase-pivot"/>
  <text x="690" y="295" text-anchor="middle" class="step-title">Step 3 — Wide-to-long pivot</text>
  <text x="690" y="315" text-anchor="middle" class="step-file">R/transform/mn26_pivot.R</text>

  <!-- Count after pivot -->
  <path d="M 690 330 L 690 380" class="flow-line" marker-end="url(#mn26arrow)"/>
  <rect x="560" y="342" width="260" height="22" rx="4" ry="4" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#fecaca"/>
  <text x="690" y="358" text-anchor="middle" class="count-badge">10,517 child-rows (10,200 + 317)</text>

  <!-- Step 4: Store raw -->
  <rect x="80" y="380" width="380" height="60" class="step-box phase-store"/>
  <text x="270" y="405" text-anchor="middle" class="step-title">Step 4 — Store raw (wide + long)</text>
  <text x="270" y="425" text-anchor="middle" class="step-file">pipelines/python/insert_raw_data.py</text>

  <!-- Step 5: Transform -->
  <rect x="500" y="380" width="380" height="60" class="step-box phase-transform"/>
  <text x="690" y="405" text-anchor="middle" class="step-title">Step 5 — Data transformation</text>
  <text x="690" y="425" text-anchor="middle" class="step-file">R/transform/mn26_transforms.R :: recode_it()</text>

  <!-- Step 3 → Step 4 (side branch) -->
  <path d="M 500 410 L 460 410" class="flow-line" marker-end="url(#mn26arrow)"/>
  <text x="480" y="395" text-anchor="middle" class="arrow-label">(raw storage)</text>

  <!-- Count after transform -->
  <path d="M 690 440 L 690 490" class="flow-line" marker-end="url(#mn26arrow)"/>
  <rect x="600" y="452" width="180" height="22" rx="4" ry="4" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#fecaca"/>
  <text x="690" y="468" text-anchor="middle" class="count-badge">10,517 × 627 cols</text>

  <!-- Step 6: Eligibility -->
  <rect x="500" y="490" width="380" height="60" class="step-box phase-eligible"/>
  <text x="690" y="515" text-anchor="middle" class="step-title">Step 6 — Eligibility validation</text>
  <text x="690" y="535" text-anchor="middle" class="step-file">R/harmonize/mn26_eligibility.R :: check_mn26_eligibility()</text>

  <!-- Count after eligibility -->
  <path d="M 690 550 L 690 600" class="flow-line" marker-end="url(#mn26arrow)"/>
  <rect x="600" y="562" width="180" height="22" rx="4" ry="4" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#fecaca"/>
  <text x="690" y="578" text-anchor="middle" class="count-badge">964 eligible (9.2%)</text>

  <!-- Step 7: Inclusion filter -->
  <rect x="500" y="600" width="380" height="60" class="step-box phase-eligible"/>
  <text x="690" y="625" text-anchor="middle" class="step-title">Step 7 — Create meets_inclusion flag</text>
  <text x="690" y="645" text-anchor="middle" class="step-file">R/harmonize/mn26_eligibility.R :: apply_mn26_inclusion()</text>

  <!-- Count after inclusion -->
  <path d="M 690 660 L 690 710" class="flow-line" marker-end="url(#mn26arrow)"/>
  <rect x="600" y="672" width="180" height="22" rx="4" ry="4" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#fecaca"/>
  <text x="690" y="688" text-anchor="middle" class="count-badge">964 meets_inclusion</text>

  <!-- Step 8: Scoring -->
  <rect x="500" y="710" width="380" height="60" class="step-box phase-score"/>
  <text x="690" y="735" text-anchor="middle" class="step-title">Step 8 — Kidsights scoring (MAP)</text>
  <text x="690" y="755" text-anchor="middle" class="step-file">KidsightsPublic::score_kidsights()</text>

  <!-- Count after scoring -->
  <path d="M 690 770 L 690 820" class="flow-line" marker-end="url(#mn26arrow)"/>
  <rect x="600" y="782" width="180" height="22" rx="4" ry="4" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#fecaca"/>
  <text x="690" y="798" text-anchor="middle" class="count-badge">848 scored (of 964)</text>

  <!-- Step 9: Store transformed -->
  <rect x="500" y="820" width="380" height="60" class="step-box phase-store"/>
  <text x="690" y="845" text-anchor="middle" class="step-title">Step 9 — Store transformed data</text>
  <text x="690" y="865" text-anchor="middle" class="step-file">pipelines/python/insert_raw_data.py → mn26_transformed</text>

  <!-- Step 10: Store dictionary -->
  <path d="M 690 880 L 690 910" class="flow-line" marker-end="url(#mn26arrow)"/>
  <rect x="500" y="910" width="380" height="60" class="step-box phase-store"/>
  <text x="690" y="935" text-anchor="middle" class="step-title">Step 10 — Store data dictionary</text>
  <text x="690" y="955" text-anchor="middle" class="step-file">pipelines/python/insert_raw_data.py → mn26_data_dictionary</text>

  <!-- External: DuckDB destination -->
  <path d="M 270 440 L 270 1040" class="flow-line"/>
  <path d="M 690 970 L 690 1010 L 270 1010 L 270 1040" class="flow-line" marker-end="url(#mn26arrow)"/>
  <rect x="120" y="1040" width="300" height="50" class="external"/>
  <text x="270" y="1062" text-anchor="middle" class="external-text">DuckDB: data/duckdb/kidsights_local.duckdb</text>
  <text x="270" y="1080" text-anchor="middle" class="step-file">mn26_raw_wide · mn26_raw · mn26_transformed · mn26_data_dictionary</text>

  <!-- Orchestrator annotation -->
  <rect x="40" y="1130" width="880" height="60" rx="6" ry="6" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#cbd5e1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/>
  <text x="480" y="1155" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#0f172a">Orchestrator: pipelines/orchestration/mn26_pipeline.R</text>
  <text x="480" y="1175" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#475569">All 10 steps coordinated in run_mn26_pipeline(). Entry point: run_mn26_pipeline.R</text>

  <!-- Time annotation -->
  <text x="480" y="1220" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#64748b">Total runtime: ~2.0 seconds (without database insertion) · ~30–60 seconds with full DB storage</text>
</svg>

<div class="mn26-legend">
  <span><span class="swatch" style="background:#dbeafe;border:1px solid #2563eb;"></span>Extraction</span>
  <span><span class="swatch" style="background:#e0f2fe;border:1px solid #0284c7;"></span>Pivot</span>
  <span><span class="swatch" style="background:#dcfce7;border:1px solid #16a34a;"></span>Transform</span>
  <span><span class="swatch" style="background:#fef3c7;border:1px solid #d97706;"></span>Eligibility</span>
  <span><span class="swatch" style="background:#fae8ff;border:1px solid #c026d3;"></span>Scoring</span>
  <span><span class="swatch" style="background:#f1f5f9;border:1px solid #64748b;"></span>Storage</span>
</div>
</div>
```

## Narrative walkthrough

The pipeline is organized into six logical phases. A reviewer can think
of each phase as answering one question.

### Extraction (Steps 1–2): "What data do we have?"

The pipeline loads REDCap API credentials from a local CSV (Step 1) and
pulls raw data plus the project data dictionary from each of the 4 NORC
REDCap projects (Step 2). Each project contributes ~2,500 records;
they're combined using `flexible_bind_rows()` which resolves column
type conflicts (e.g., timestamps that are character in some projects
and datetime in others). Output: **10,200 wide-format records**.

### Pivot (Step 3): "One row per child"

MN26 is a **multi-child survey** — each household can enroll up to 2
children, stored in wide format (columns like `cqr009_c2`,
`age_in_days_c2_n`). The pivot expands the wide data into long format
with one row per child, identified by `pid + record_id + child_num`.
Households with no child-2 data stay as single rows. Output:
**10,517 child rows** = 10,200 child-1 + 317 child-2.

### Transform (Step 5): "Recode raw into derived variables"

`recode_it(what = "all")` dispatches through 10 transformation
categories (race, sex, age, education, income, geographic, mental
health, childcare, caregiver relationship, eligibility). Each category
reads raw REDCap fields and produces derived analysis-ready variables.
See [Variable Recoding Reference](#sec-recoding) for the mapping from
raw fields to derived columns. Output: same 10,517 rows, expanded from
883 raw columns to ~627 (many raw columns collapse into derived ones).

### Eligibility (Steps 6–7): "Who counts?"

Because NORC pre-screens participants before they reach REDCap, only
two criteria are checked on-REDCap: informed consent (`eq001 == 1`)
and child age 0–5 years (`age_in_days_n <= 1825`). The legacy NE25
criteria (`eq002`, `eq003`, `mn_eqstate`) are absent from production
exports and automatically skipped. Output: **964 eligible children**
(9.2% of 10,517). For MN26, `meets_inclusion` equals `eligible` with no
additional filters.

### Scoring (Step 8): "What's their developmental score?"

Only eligible children are passed to `KidsightsPublic::score_kidsights()`,
which runs a CmdStan MAP optimization with fixed item parameters from
the 2023 Mplus calibration. Children with fewer than 5 non-missing item
responses are not scored (`min_responses = 5`). Output: **848 scored**
children (88% of eligible).

### Storage (Steps 4, 9–10): "Persist for analysis"

Three storage steps write to DuckDB via a Python helper
(`pipelines/python/insert_raw_data.py`):

- **Step 4**: raw wide format (audit trail) + raw long format (working copy)
- **Step 9**: fully transformed + scored data → `mn26_transformed`
- **Step 10**: REDCap data dictionary → `mn26_data_dictionary`

The `--skip-database` CLI flag suppresses all three. Database steps are
the slowest part of the pipeline (file I/O + Python subprocess) —
skipping them drops total runtime from ~30–60 seconds to ~2 seconds.

# Setup & Prerequisites {#sec-setup}

## System requirements

| Requirement | Tested version | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| R | 4.5.1 | Windows build at `C:/Program Files/R/R-4.5.1/bin/` |
| Python | 3.13+ | Only needed if you want database insertion (Steps 4 and 9) |
| CmdStan | latest via `cmdstanr` | Required for Kidsights scoring |
| DuckDB | bundled with R `duckdb` package | Only needed for Step 9 database insertion |

This pipeline runs on Windows with Unix-style paths (forward slashes).
Commands use `Rscript.exe` explicitly to avoid PATH ambiguity.

## R package dependencies

The pipeline script sources each dependency file directly at startup
(no `DESCRIPTION` / no `renv.lock`). The required CRAN packages are:

```{.r}
# Core pipeline
install.packages(c(
  "dplyr", "tidyr", "stringr",   # data manipulation
  "yaml",                          # config file parsing
  "arrow",                         # feather file I/O (for database handoff)
  "REDCapR",                       # REDCap API extraction
  "httr",                          # HTTP for dictionary retrieval
  "plyr",                          # mapvalues() in recode_it (LOAD BEFORE dplyr)
  "labelled",                      # variable labels
  "lubridate",                     # date arithmetic (CPI/FPL)
  "fredr"                          # FRED API for CPI ratios
))
```

Additional packages used only by specific transform categories:

```{.r}
install.packages(c(
  "stringdist",                    # fuzzy matching in reconciliation_audit.R
  "htmltools"                      # audit report rendering
))
```

## Installing KidsightsPublic (required for scoring)

The pipeline's Step 8 (Kidsights developmental scoring) calls
`KidsightsPublic::score_kidsights()`. This package ships the item
parameters from the 2023 Mplus calibration and the CmdStan models for
MAP scoring. Install from GitHub:

```{.r}
# Install dev tools if needed
install.packages("devtools")

# Install KidsightsPublic
devtools::install_github("marcus-waldman/KidsightsPublic")

# Install CmdStan (one-time setup, ~15 minutes)
cmdstanr::install_cmdstan()
```

Verify both are working:

```{.r}
library(KidsightsPublic)
cmdstanr::cmdstan_version()  # should print e.g., "2.35.0"
```

::: {.callout-warning}
If `cmdstanr::install_cmdstan()` fails with compiler errors, you may need
to install Rtools (Windows) or a C++ toolchain. The CmdStan maintainers
keep up-to-date setup instructions at
<https://mc-stan.org/cmdstanr/articles/cmdstanr.html>.
:::

## REDCap API credentials

MN26 uses **4 production REDCap projects** hosted on the UNMC REDCap
server. Access requires individual API tokens — one per project.

### Credentials file format

Create a CSV at `C:/my_auths/kidsights_redcap_norc_MN_2026.csv` with
three columns:

```
project,pid,api_code
Kidsights Survey NORC 1,8723,<64-char hex token>
Kidsights Survey NORC 2,8724,<64-char hex token>
Kidsights Survey NORC 3,8725,<64-char hex token>
Kidsights Survey NORC 4,8726,<64-char hex token>
```

- `project`: descriptive name; propagated as `source_project` in output
- `pid`: REDCap project ID (integer)
- `api_code`: API token (alphanumeric, typically 64 characters)

### Where the pipeline looks for credentials

The pipeline resolves credentials in this order:

1. `--credentials <path>` CLI flag on `run_mn26_pipeline.R`
2. `REDCAP_API_CREDENTIALS_PATH_MN26` environment variable (set via `.env`)
3. `api_credentials_file` field in `config/sources/mn26.yaml`
   (default: `C:/my_auths/kidsights_redcap_norc_MN_2026.csv`)

::: {.callout-important}
**Never commit the credentials CSV to git.** The repository `.gitignore`
excludes `*api*.csv`, but the safest practice is to keep credentials in a
separate directory outside the repo (e.g., `C:/my_auths/`). Each
collaborator maintains their own copy.
:::

## Directory layout expected at runtime

The pipeline is designed to be run from the **project root**, not from
`pipelines/` or any subdirectory. Relative `source()` calls depend on this.

```
Kidsights-Data-Platform/              <- run commands from here
├── R/
│   ├── extract/mn26.R               <- extraction functions
│   ├── transform/mn26_pivot.R       <- wide-to-long
│   ├── transform/mn26_transforms.R  <- recode_it() dispatcher
│   ├── harmonize/mn26_eligibility.R <- 4-criteria check
│   └── utils/                       <- shared helpers
├── config/sources/mn26.yaml         <- config
├── pipelines/orchestration/
│   └── mn26_pipeline.R              <- 10-step orchestrator
├── schemas/landing/mn26.sql         <- DuckDB schema
├── run_mn26_pipeline.R              <- entry point
└── data/duckdb/                     <- output DB (created on first run)
```

# Running the Pipeline {#sec-running}

## Entry point

The top-level script is `run_mn26_pipeline.R`. It parses command-line
flags, sources the orchestrator, and calls `run_mn26_pipeline()`.

## Supported flags

| Flag | Effect |
|---|---|
| `--skip-database` | Skip Steps 4, 9 (wide storage, long storage, dictionary storage). Transforms, eligibility, and scoring still run, but nothing writes to DuckDB. |
| `--credentials <path>` | Override the credentials CSV path. Takes precedence over env var and config. |
| (no flags) | Full pipeline including database insertion. |

## Command examples

**First run / dry run** (recommended for new reviewers):

```bash
"C:/Program Files/R/R-4.5.1/bin/Rscript.exe" run_mn26_pipeline.R --skip-database
```

**Full run with database insertion:**

```bash
"C:/Program Files/R/R-4.5.1/bin/Rscript.exe" run_mn26_pipeline.R
```

**Run against a test/dev credentials file:**

```bash
"C:/Program Files/R/R-4.5.1/bin/Rscript.exe" run_mn26_pipeline.R \
  --credentials "C:/my_auths/kidsights_redcap_norc_test_MN.csv" \
  --skip-database
```

**Interactive run from an R session** (useful for debugging; gives you
access to the intermediate `result` object):

```{.r}
source("pipelines/orchestration/mn26_pipeline.R")
result <- run_mn26_pipeline(
  config_path = "config/sources/mn26.yaml",
  skip_database = TRUE
)

# Inspect stages
head(result$raw_wide)   # pre-pivot
head(result$raw_long)   # post-pivot
head(result$data)       # final transformed + scored
result$metrics          # step-by-step timing and counts
```

## Reading the console output

Each step prints a banner and key metrics. The format is:

```
--- Step N: <Step name> ---
  <progress message or metric>
  [OK] <success indicator>  OR  [WARN] <non-fatal issue>
```

Here's what healthy output looks like for each step, annotated:

```
--- Step 1: Loading API Credentials ---
[OK] Loaded 4 MN26 API credential(s)             <- matches rows in CSV

--- Step 2: Extracting REDCap Data ---
  Extracting: Kidsights Survey NORC 1 (PID 8723)
    [OK] 2500 records, 883 columns                <- one line per project
  Extracting: Kidsights Survey NORC 2 (PID 8724)
    [OK] 2500 records, 883 columns
  ... (projects 3, 4)
[OK] MN26 extraction complete: 10200 records, 883 columns

--- Step 3: Wide-to-Long Pivot ---
MN26 Pivot: 10200 household records
  Child 2 columns detected: 368                   <- identifies _c2 columns
  Mappable to child 1: 329                        <- have a matching base column
  Child-2-only columns: 39                        <- no NE25 equivalent
  Child 1 rows: 10200
  Households with child 2: 317 of 10200           <- ~3% multi-child
  Final long format: 10517 rows                   <- 10200 + 317

--- Step 4: SKIPPED (skip_database=TRUE) ---      <- or would store to DB

--- Step 5: Data Transformation ---
  Transformed: 10517 records, 627 columns         <- derived vars added

--- Step 6: Eligibility Validation ---
Checking MN26 eligibility (NORC production-aligned)...
  Total children:      10517
  Eligible:            964 (9.2%)
  Criterion breakdown:
    Consent (eq001):         1217 [required]
    Child age (<=5yr):       964 [required]
    Parent age (eq003):      column absent [NORC pre-screened, skipped]
    Primary CG (eq002):      column absent [NORC pre-screened, skipped]
    MN residence (mn_eqstate): column absent [NORC pre-screened, skipped]

--- Step 7: Inclusion Filter ---
  Meets inclusion: 964 of 10517                   <- = eligible for MN26

--- Step 8: Kidsights Developmental Scoring ---
  Scoring 964 eligible children (of 10517 total)...
Initial log joint probability = -44379.1
Optimization terminated normally:
  Convergence detected: relative change in objective function was below tolerance
Finished in  0.7 seconds.
  Scored: 848 of 964 eligible children           <- ~12% short of 5-item min

--- Step 9: SKIPPED (skip_database=TRUE) ---

========================================
MN26 Pipeline Complete                           <- summary banner
========================================
```

## What to verify after a run

A reviewer running the pipeline end-to-end should check these four things:

1. **Extraction count matches REDCap.** Compare `Records extracted` with
   a direct query to each REDCap project (record count should match
   exactly — this pipeline does no filtering during extraction).
2. **Pivot row count is correct.** `records_after_pivot == records_extracted + households_with_child2`.
3. **Eligibility is plausible.** `Eligible` should be between ~5% and
   ~15% of extracted during early data collection and converge higher
   as recruitment matures.
4. **Scoring attrition is reasonable.** `Scored / Eligible` should be
   >80%. If it drops significantly below that, the item responses in
   REDCap may have changed encoding (see [troubleshooting](#sec-troubleshooting)).

# Code Organization Map {#sec-code-map}

The MN26 pipeline is organized into single-responsibility modules. This
section tells you **which file to open for any given concern**.

## File-by-file responsibilities

| File | Lines | Responsibility | Key exports |
|---|---:|---|---|
| `run_mn26_pipeline.R` | 41 | Entry point. Parses CLI flags, sources the orchestrator. | — |
| `pipelines/orchestration/mn26_pipeline.R` | 344 | 10-step orchestrator. Coordinates all other modules. | `run_mn26_pipeline()` |
| `config/sources/mn26.yaml` | 188 | Pipeline configuration (REDCap URL, credentials path, instruments, validation rules). | — |
| `R/extract/mn26.R` | 235 | REDCap API extraction. Loads credentials, pulls data + dictionary from N projects. | `load_mn26_credentials()`, `extract_mn26_data()`, `extract_mn26_dictionary()` |
| `R/transform/mn26_pivot.R` | 207 | Wide-to-long pivot for multi-child households. | `pivot_mn26_wide_to_long()` |
| `R/transform/mn26_transforms.R` | 1390 | Variable recoding dispatcher (race, sex, age, education, income, etc.). | `recode_it()`, `recode__()` (internal) |
| `R/harmonize/mn26_eligibility.R` | 157 | 4-criteria eligibility check (NORC production-aligned). | `check_mn26_eligibility()`, `apply_mn26_inclusion()` |
| `R/utils/flexible_bind_rows.R` | 92 | Resolves column type conflicts when combining multi-project REDCap extracts. | `flexible_bind_rows()` |
| `R/utils/recode_utils.R` | 23 | Recode sentinel missing codes (99, 9, -99, etc.) to NA. | `recode_missing()` |
| `R/utils/cpi_utils.R` | 49 | CPI adjustment ratios via FRED API (for income deflation). | `cpi_ratio_1999()` |
| `R/utils/poverty_utils.R` | 69 | Federal poverty thresholds 2020–2026. | `get_poverty_threshold()` |
| `R/utils/safe_joins.R` | 95 | Safe `left_join` wrapper (validates row count, detects column collisions). | `safe_left_join()` |
| `schemas/landing/mn26.sql` | 239 | DuckDB table schemas for `mn26_raw_wide`, `mn26_raw`, `mn26_transformed`, `mn26_data_dictionary`. | — |

## Pipeline step → file mapping

Each of the 10 orchestrator steps calls into specific modules:

| Step | What it does | Primary file | Key function |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Load API credentials | `R/extract/mn26.R` | `load_mn26_credentials()` |
| 2 | Extract REDCap data + dictionary | `R/extract/mn26.R` | `extract_mn26_data()` |
| 3 | Wide-to-long pivot | `R/transform/mn26_pivot.R` | `pivot_mn26_wide_to_long()` |
| 4 | Store raw (wide + long) in DuckDB | `pipelines/python/insert_raw_data.py` (via `system2()`) | — |
| 5 | Variable transformation | `R/transform/mn26_transforms.R` | `recode_it(data, dict, what = "all")` |
| 6 | Eligibility validation | `R/harmonize/mn26_eligibility.R` | `check_mn26_eligibility()` |
| 7 | Create `meets_inclusion` filter | `R/harmonize/mn26_eligibility.R` | `apply_mn26_inclusion()` |
| 8 | Kidsights developmental scoring | `KidsightsPublic` package | `KidsightsPublic::score_kidsights()` |
| 9 | Store transformed data in DuckDB | `pipelines/python/insert_raw_data.py` | — |
| 10 | Store data dictionary in DuckDB | `pipelines/python/insert_raw_data.py` | — |

## Relationship to the NE25 pipeline

The MN26 pipeline is a **sibling** of the NE25 pipeline, not a fork.
Shared utilities live in `R/utils/` and are sourced by both:

- `R/utils/flexible_bind_rows.R` — both extract modules use this
- `R/utils/recode_utils.R` — both transforms use this
- `R/utils/cpi_utils.R` — both use this for income deflation
- `R/utils/poverty_utils.R` — both use this for FPL calculation
- `R/utils/safe_joins.R` — both use this for joins

Study-specific code (extraction, transforms, eligibility, orchestrator)
is duplicated per study. Duplication is intentional: the two studies have
enough differences in raw field names and eligibility logic that a
single "generic" version would need too many study flags to be readable.

::: {.callout-tip}
**If you're coming from the NE25 pipeline** and are looking for the MN26
equivalent of a file, the naming is consistent:

- `R/extract/ne25.R` → `R/extract/mn26.R`
- `R/transform/ne25_transforms.R` → `R/transform/mn26_transforms.R`
- `R/harmonize/ne25_eligibility.R` → `R/harmonize/mn26_eligibility.R`
- `pipelines/orchestration/ne25_pipeline.R` → `pipelines/orchestration/mn26_pipeline.R`
:::

## Navigation by concern

If you're reviewing for…

- **Credentials handling or API calls** → `R/extract/mn26.R`
- **Multi-child pivot correctness** → `R/transform/mn26_pivot.R`
- **Race / sex / age / income recoding** → `R/transform/mn26_transforms.R` (see [Variable Recoding Reference](#sec-recoding) for function line numbers)
- **Who counts as "eligible"** → `R/harmonize/mn26_eligibility.R`
- **How the pipeline decides what to run** → `pipelines/orchestration/mn26_pipeline.R`
- **What tables exist in the database** → `schemas/landing/mn26.sql`
- **Configuration knobs (URLs, file paths, thresholds)** → `config/sources/mn26.yaml`

# Variable Recoding Reference {#sec-recoding}

This section documents the **key derived variables** that a code
reviewer is most likely to audit for recoding accuracy. All transforms
live in `R/transform/mn26_transforms.R` and are dispatched by the
`recode_it()` function (line 1359) via the internal `recode__()` helper
(line 297).

::: {.callout-note}
The recoding is intentionally a direct adaptation of the NE25 pipeline
transforms. The reconciliation audit
(`scripts/mn26/reconciliation_audit.R`) established that 459 of 471
shared fields are **identical** between studies — so most transforms
port directly. Changes concentrate in 4 sections: race, caregiver
relationship, age, and income.
:::

## How recode_it() dispatches transforms

`recode_it()` calls `recode__()` for each category in this list when
invoked with `what = "all"`:

```{.r}
c("include", "race", "caregiver relationship", "education",
  "sex", "age", "income", "geographic", "mental health", "childcare")
```

Each category is a self-contained branch inside `recode__()`. The table
below maps each **key derived variable** to the raw REDCap field(s) it's
derived from, the line range in `R/transform/mn26_transforms.R`, and any
NE25↔MN26 differences a reviewer should check.

## Key derived variables

### Child sex

| Aspect | Value |
|---|---|
| Source REDCap field | `cqr009` |
| Transform branch | `recode__()` lines 460–487 (`what == "sex"`) |
| Output variables | `sex` (factor with "Female"/"Male"), `female` (logical) |
| Coding | `1 = Female, 0 = Male` — **same in NE25 and MN26** |
| Approach | Dictionary-driven: `plyr::mapvalues()` using labels from REDCap dictionary |

::: {.callout-important}
The sex coding is **identical** between NE25 and MN26 despite earlier
documentation claims of a swap. Both studies code `1 = Female`,
`0 = Male` in the REDCap dictionary. See reconciliation audit output
for confirmation; the kidsights-norc repo was updated to remove the
incorrect "swapped" claim.
:::

---

### Child race and ethnicity

| Aspect | Value |
|---|---|
| Source REDCap fields | `cqr010b___100` through `cqr010b___105` (6 checkboxes) + `cqr011` (Hispanic) |
| Transform branch | `recode__()` lines 333–410 (`what %in% c("race", "ethnicity")`) |
| Output variables | `race` (factor), `hisp` (factor), `raceG` (combined) |
| NE25 vs MN26 | **STRUCTURAL CHANGE**: NE25 had `cqr010___1..15` (15 granular categories that got collapsed in code). MN26 has `cqr010b___100..105` — 6 pre-collapsed categories. **No in-R collapse needed for MN26.** |
| Checkbox decoding | Each `cqr010b___NNN` column is 0/1 for that race being selected. Multiple selections → "Two or More Races". |
| Race code map | 100=AIAN, 101=Asian, 102=Black, 103=NHPI, 104=White, 105=Other |

---

### Caregiver race and ethnicity

| Aspect | Value |
|---|---|
| Source REDCap fields | `sq002b___100` through `sq002b___105` + `sq003` |
| Transform branch | Same branch as child race, lines 333–410 |
| Output variables | `a1_race`, `a1_hisp`, `a1_raceG` (parent/caregiver versions) |
| NE25 vs MN26 | **STRUCTURAL CHANGE**: same collapse-in-REDCap pattern as child race (`sq002___*` → `sq002b___*`). |

---

### Child age

| Aspect | Value |
|---|---|
| Source REDCap field | `age_in_days_n` (calculated field: `datediff([dob_n], [consent_date_n], 'd')`) |
| Transform branch | `recode__()` lines 489–508 (`what == "age"`) |
| Output variables | `years_old` (= `age_in_days_n / 365.25`), `months_old`, `days_old` (= `age_in_days_n`) |
| NE25 vs MN26 | **RENAMED**: NE25's `age_in_days` → MN26's `age_in_days_n`. Same calculation underneath; only the field name changed. |

---

### Caregiver age

| Aspect | Value |
|---|---|
| Source REDCap field | `cqr003` |
| Transform branch | Same `age` branch, lines 489–508 |
| Output variable | `a1_years_old` |
| Coding | Direct numeric passthrough |

---

### Parent gender

| Aspect | Value |
|---|---|
| Source REDCap field | `mn2` |
| Transform branch | `recode__()` lines 411–458 (`what %in% c("caregiver relationship")`) |
| Output variable | `female_a1` (logical) |
| NE25 vs MN26 | **RENAMED + EXPANDED**: NE25's `cqr002` → MN26's `mn2`. NE25 was binary (0/1); MN26 adds non-binary code 97. Transform uses `mn2 == 0` (Female), so non-binary respondents get `female_a1 = FALSE`. |

---

### Education

| Aspect | Value |
|---|---|
| Source REDCap fields | `cqr004` (primary caregiver), `nschj017` (secondary caregiver, if present) |
| Transform branch | `recode__()` lines 555–700 (`what %in% c("education")`) |
| Output variables | `educ_a1`, `educ_a2` (8-category); `educ4_a1`, `educ4_a2` (4-category); `educ6_a1`, `educ6_a2` (6-category); `educ_mom`, `educ4_mom`, `educ6_mom`, `educ_max` |
| Coding | **Identical in both studies**: 0 = 8th grade or less, …, 8 = Doctorate/Professional. |
| Approach | Dictionary-driven: factor levels read from REDCap dictionary, so reclassification into 4- and 6-category versions is resilient to label changes. |
| Category collapses | 8→4: (0,1)=Less than HS, (2)=HS, (3,4,5)=Some College, (6,7,8)=College+. 8→6: splits "College+" into Bachelor's, Master's, Doctorate. |

---

### Income & federal poverty level (FPL)

| Aspect | Value |
|---|---|
| Source REDCap fields | `cqr006` (family income), `consent_date_n` (for CPI adjustment), `fqlive1_1` + `fqlive1_2` (family size) |
| Transform branch | `recode__()` lines 510–553 (`what == "income"`) |
| Output variables | `income` (nominal $), `cpi99` (CPI ratio), `inc99` (1999 dollars), `family_size`, `federal_poverty_threshold`, `fpl` (% of FPL), `fplcat` (factor: <100%, 100–199%, 200–299%, 300–399%, 400+%) |
| NE25 vs MN26 | **RENAMED**: NE25's `consent_date` → MN26's `consent_date_n`. **RECODED**: `cqr006` slider range changed — NE25 max was $500,000, MN26 max is $250,000+ (slider clipped at 250K with "+" label). Family size formula unchanged. |
| External calls | `cpi_ratio_1999()` from `R/utils/cpi_utils.R` (FRED API), `get_poverty_threshold()` from `R/utils/poverty_utils.R` (hardcoded HHS guidelines 2020–2026) |

::: {.callout-warning}
The `cqr006` recode is the one transform most likely to produce subtly
different distributions between NE25 and MN26. Households earning
$250K–$500K in NE25 land at their actual value; in MN26, they're all
clipped to 250K. A reviewer looking at FPL distributions for high-income
families should expect a mass point at 250K in MN26 that isn't there in
NE25.
:::

---

### Marital status

| Aspect | Value |
|---|---|
| Source REDCap field | `cqfa001` |
| Transform branch | Not directly in `recode__()`; labeled factor is generated by the dictionary value_labels at load time |
| Output variable | `cqfa001` (labeled factor): 0=Married, 1=Not married but living with partner, 2=Never married, 3=Divorced, 4=Separated, 5=Widowed |
| NE25 vs MN26 | **Coding identical.** Earlier documentation claimed a shift (NE25 1–6, MN26 0–5), but reconciliation audit confirmed both studies use 0–5. |

---

### Eligibility & inclusion

| Aspect | Value |
|---|---|
| Source REDCap fields | `eq001` (consent), `age_in_days_n` (child age); optionally `eq002`, `eq003`, `mn_eqstate` |
| Function | `check_mn26_eligibility()` + `apply_mn26_inclusion()` in `R/harmonize/mn26_eligibility.R` |
| Output variables | `pass_consent`, `pass_child_age`, `pass_parent_age`, `pass_primary_caregiver`, `pass_minnesota_residence`, `eligible`, `meets_inclusion`, `exclusion_reason` |
| NE25 vs MN26 | **ARCHITECTURALLY DIFFERENT**. See [Eligibility Logic](#sec-eligibility) for full details. Required: `eq001 == 1` AND `age_in_days_n <= 1825`. Optional (if columns present): `eq002/eq003/mn_eqstate`. NE25 required all 9 CID checks; MN26 offloads caregiver screening to NORC. |

---

## How to spot-check a recode

A good audit pattern:

1. Pick a raw REDCap variable and its expected output (e.g., `cqr009` → `female`).
2. Run the pipeline with `--skip-database` and capture the result:

```{.r}
source("pipelines/orchestration/mn26_pipeline.R")
result <- run_mn26_pipeline(skip_database = TRUE)
```

3. Cross-tab the raw and derived columns:

```{.r}
# Sex check: cqr009 codes 1=Female, 0=Male
table(result$data$cqr009, result$data$female, useNA = "always")
```

4. Expected output for a correct transform:

```
        FALSE  TRUE  <NA>
   0    XXXX    0     0
   1      0   YYYY    0
   <NA>   0     0    ZZZZ
```

5. Investigate any off-diagonal values — they indicate a miscoding.

::: {.callout-tip}
The `recode_missing()` utility in `R/utils/recode_utils.R` converts
sentinel missing codes (99, 9, -99, 999, etc.) to NA **before** any
arithmetic on composite variables. This is critical for composite
scores (PHQ-2 total, GAD-2 total, ACE totals) and is a common source of
recoding bugs when it's missed. Search for `recode_missing(` in the
transforms file to find all uses.
:::

# Eligibility Logic {#sec-eligibility}

The MN26 eligibility logic differs from NE25 in one critical way:
**NORC pre-screens participants before they reach REDCap**. This has
consequences for which fields are collected, which criteria the pipeline
can check, and how the pipeline adapts to partial data.

## Production architecture

```
Prospective participant
        │
        ▼
┌──────────────────────┐
│ NORC pre-screening   │  ← caregiver status, age ≥19, Minnesota residence
│ (happens off-REDCap) │    screened HERE
└──────────────────────┘
        │
        ▼ (approved participants only)
┌──────────────────────┐
│ REDCap survey        │  ← consent, child age, developmental items
│ (what this pipeline  │    collected HERE
│  sees)               │
└──────────────────────┘
```

Because NORC vets caregiver age, primary caregiver status, and state
residence **before** routing participants to the Kidsights instrument,
the legacy NE25 eligibility fields (`eq002`, `eq003`, `mn_eqstate`) are
not administered. They're present in the REDCap data dictionary (as
dormant legacy fields from the NE25 instrument template), but they're
**not exported in the production data** because no records ever have
values for them.

## What the pipeline checks

The pipeline's eligibility function (`check_mn26_eligibility()` in
`R/harmonize/mn26_eligibility.R`) is adaptive: it always checks the
required criteria, and conditionally applies legacy criteria if the
columns happen to be present.

### Required criteria (always checked)

| # | Criterion | Field | Rule |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Informed consent | `eq001` | `eq001 == 1` |
| 2 | Child age 0–5 | `age_in_days_n` | `age_in_days_n <= 1825` |

If either required column is missing from the data, the function raises
an error immediately — this is a structural problem, not a data problem.

### Optional criteria (checked only if columns present)

| # | Criterion | Field | Status in production |
|---|---|---|---|
| 3 | Caregiver age ≥19 | `eq003` | **Absent** — NORC pre-screened |
| 4 | Primary caregiver | `eq002` | **Absent** — NORC pre-screened |
| 5 | Minnesota residence | `mn_eqstate` | **Absent** — NORC pre-screened |

If a column is absent, the function logs `column absent [NORC pre-screened, skipped]`
in its verbose output and treats that criterion as effectively passed.
This is safe because NORC only routes approved participants to REDCap.

### Final `eligible` flag

```
eligible = pass_consent
         & pass_child_age
         & (pass_parent_age          | is.na(pass_parent_age))
         & (pass_primary_caregiver   | is.na(pass_primary_caregiver))
         & (pass_minnesota_residence | is.na(pass_minnesota_residence))
```

The `| is.na(...)` clauses mean "if this criterion is missing entirely,
don't count it against the participant". Legacy fields contribute NA
rather than FALSE, so they short-circuit correctly.

## Inclusion filter

`apply_mn26_inclusion()` copies `eligible` into `meets_inclusion`. For
MN26, no additional filters (authenticity screening, influential
observation flagging) are applied — that differs from NE25, which layers
those checks on top of eligibility.

## Historical context

The design decision to offload eligibility to NORC is documented in the
sibling repository
[`kidsights-norc`](https://github.com/marcus-waldman/kidsights-norc),
specifically commit `4d97629` ("Restructure monitoring output per NORC
feedback"). That commit dropped the on-REDCap eligibility evaluation
from the monitoring tool entirely, replacing it with a raw-field dump.
Our pipeline takes the intermediate position: check what we can
(consent + age), trust NORC for the rest.

---

# Scoring {#sec-scoring}

The Kidsights developmental score is produced by
`KidsightsPublic::score_kidsights()`, an R package that wraps a CmdStan
model for MAP (maximum a posteriori) IRT scoring with fixed item
parameters from the 2023 Mplus calibration.

::: {.callout-note}
**For a reviewer focused on recoding accuracy**: you can treat
`score_kidsights()` as a black box. The pipeline's responsibility is to
(1) give it the correct inputs (eligible children with valid ages and
item responses) and (2) join the output back correctly. The scoring
math itself lives in the KidsightsPublic package and is out of scope
for this document.
:::

## What gets scored

**Input**: rows from `transformed_data` where `meets_inclusion == TRUE`.

**Required columns per row**:

- `pid`, `record_id`, `child_num` — composite identifier
- `years_old` — child's age in years (used by the age-informed prior)
- 264 developmental item columns (names follow the MN26 lexicon, e.g.,
  `c020`, `nom001`, `cahmi22_001`); the package auto-detects the lexicon

## How scoring is called

In `pipelines/orchestration/mn26_pipeline.R` (Step 8):

```{.r}
# Filter to eligible records first (score_kidsights requires valid years_old)
scoring_subset <- transformed_data %>%
  dplyr::filter(isTRUE(meets_inclusion) | meets_inclusion %in% TRUE)

# Run MAP scoring
kidsights_scores <- KidsightsPublic::score_kidsights(
  scoring_subset,
  id_cols = c("pid", "record_id", "child_num"),
  min_responses = 5
)

# Join back; non-eligible records get NA theta
transformed_data <- transformed_data %>%
  safe_left_join(
    kidsights_scores %>% dplyr::rename(kidsights_theta = theta),
    by_vars = c("pid", "record_id", "child_num")
  )
```

## Why filter before scoring?

`score_kidsights()` requires `years_old` to be non-missing for every row
passed in (the age-informed prior needs a valid age). Non-eligible
records frequently have missing ages, either because they haven't
progressed past the screening instruments or because their `dob_n` is
still being entered. Passing them in would cause the scoring call to
error.

Filtering to `meets_inclusion == TRUE` before scoring means:

- Scoring only runs on records that passed consent + valid child age
- The joined `kidsights_theta` is `NA` for everyone else, matching their
  `NA` `meets_inclusion` status
- Attrition is transparent: `Eligible - Scored` = children who passed
  eligibility but didn't meet the `min_responses = 5` threshold

## Expected attrition

On the latest production run:

| Stage | Count | Note |
|---|---:|---|
| Extracted | 10,200 | Records from all 4 REDCap projects |
| After pivot | 10,517 | + 317 child-2 rows |
| Eligible | 964 | Passed consent + child age |
| **Scored** | **848** | ≥5 item responses; 12% attrition from eligible |

The 116 eligible-but-unscored children are partial completions —
consented and age-valid, but with fewer than 5 developmental items
answered. This is expected during early recruitment when many
participants have just started the survey.

## Output variable

`kidsights_theta` is added to `transformed_data` after scoring. It's on
the latent IRT scale (approximately N(0,1) centered). Higher values
indicate more advanced development relative to other children of the
same age. The distribution is documented in the KidsightsPublic
vignette.

---

# Troubleshooting {#sec-troubleshooting}

## "MN26 API credentials file not found"

```
Error in load_mn26_credentials() :
  MN26 API credentials file not found. Set REDCAP_API_CREDENTIALS_PATH_MN26
  in .env or provide csv_path argument.
```

**Cause**: The credentials CSV doesn't exist at the expected path.

**Fix**: Verify the file exists:

```bash
ls "C:/my_auths/kidsights_redcap_norc_MN_2026.csv"
```

If the path is different, override via the CLI:

```bash
"C:/Program Files/R/R-4.5.1/bin/Rscript.exe" run_mn26_pipeline.R \
  --credentials "C:/actual/path/to/creds.csv"
```

Or edit `config/sources/mn26.yaml` line 14:

```{.yaml}
api_credentials_file: "C:/actual/path/to/creds.csv"
```

---

## "Can't combine `..1$foo` <character> and `..2$foo` <datetime<UTC>>"

**Cause**: Before the `flexible_bind_rows` fix, a `dplyr::bind_rows()`
across multi-project extracts failed when a timestamp column was a
character string in some projects (empty, no completions yet) and a
datetime in others. Fixed in commit `2a27259` by switching to
`flexible_bind_rows()`, which promotes all conflicting columns to the
highest shared type (character wins over datetime).

**If you see this now**: it means a new column type conflict has
appeared that isn't covered by `flexible_bind_rows`. Check
`R/utils/flexible_bind_rows.R` — the type hierarchy is defined there
and you may need to add a new type mapping.

---

## "Required column 'eq001' not found in data"

**Cause**: The REDCap export no longer includes `eq001` (informed
consent). This is a structural problem — the pipeline cannot proceed
without it.

**Fix**: Verify with NORC that `eq001` is still in the `consent_doc`
instrument and being collected. If it's been renamed, update
`R/harmonize/mn26_eligibility.R` to reference the new name.

---

## "`years_old` contains missing values" during scoring

**Cause**: `score_kidsights()` received records where age could not be
computed. This usually means the eligibility filter in Step 8 is not
being applied — every record passed in should have
`meets_inclusion == TRUE`.

**Fix**: Check that the orchestrator's Step 8 is filtering before
calling `score_kidsights()`:

```{.r}
scoring_subset <- transformed_data %>%
  dplyr::filter(isTRUE(meets_inclusion) | meets_inclusion %in% TRUE)
```

If filtering is correct but some `meets_inclusion == TRUE` records
still have missing `years_old`, investigate the age transform
(`R/transform/mn26_transforms.R` lines 489–508).

---

## Scored count is dramatically lower than eligible count

**Example**: 964 eligible, only 200 scored.

**Cause**: Developmental item responses have been encoded in an
unexpected way that `score_kidsights()` can't parse. The package applies
a `min_responses = 5` threshold — if items are being marked as missing
when they shouldn't be, many eligible children fall below that cutoff.

**Diagnostic**:

```{.r}
# Count non-missing item responses per eligible child
result <- run_mn26_pipeline(skip_database = TRUE)
eligible <- result$data %>% dplyr::filter(meets_inclusion == TRUE)

# Count non-missing values in item columns (c020, c023, ...)
item_cols <- grep("^c[0-9]{3}", names(eligible), value = TRUE)
eligible$n_items <- rowSums(!is.na(eligible[, item_cols]))
hist(eligible$n_items, main = "Item responses per eligible child")
```

If the histogram shows a mass near 0, items aren't being extracted
properly. If it shows a healthy spread with most children having 20+
items, the issue is elsewhere.

---

## CmdStan compilation errors

```
Error: CmdStan installation not found
```

**Fix**:

```{.r}
cmdstanr::install_cmdstan()
```

If that fails with compiler errors, install Rtools44 (Windows):
<https://cran.r-project.org/bin/windows/Rtools/>.

---

## Pipeline runs but row counts look wrong

**Symptom**: Records-after-pivot ≠ records-extracted + households-with-child-2.

**Cause**: The pivot detected a column that looks like a child-2
suffix but doesn't belong to a child-2 instrument (false positive). Or
the `dob_c2_n` detection missed some households.

**Diagnostic**: Interactive run with verbose output:

```{.r}
source("pipelines/orchestration/mn26_pipeline.R")
result <- run_mn26_pipeline(skip_database = TRUE)

# Compare pivot metrics
result$metrics$n_child1  # should equal records extracted
result$metrics$n_child2  # should equal households with non-empty dob_c2_n
```

Compare with what you expect from the REDCap project. If they don't
match, the pivot logic in `R/transform/mn26_pivot.R` may need to
account for a new column naming pattern that NORC has introduced.

# Known Limitations {#sec-known-limitations}

As of the first successful end-to-end live run (2026-05-11, commits
`deb1f82` + `21c2863`), Step 5 (`recode_it()`) emits three
`Failed to process` warnings. The pipeline continues — the `tryCatch`
in `recode_it()` swallows individual transform errors and downstream
scoring runs cleanly on the 1,630 `meets_inclusion` records. But
about **25 derived columns** aren't populated in the transformed
output. These are documented and **deferred** because no downstream
consumer needs them yet (MN26 raking + imputation are themselves
deferred pending MN ACS extraction).

All three were pre-existing — the 2026-04-27 "scoring complete"
milestone didn't surface them because that run was synthetic-only,
and the synthetic test populates the affected fields with non-NA
values across all label ranges.

## 1. `geographic` — missing input data

**Symptom:** `Failed to process geographic :` (empty error message)

**Root cause:** The geographic transform queries the
`geo_zip_to_puma`, `geo_zip_to_county`, and `geo_zip_to_tract`
crosswalk tables. These are **Nebraska-scoped** and don't include
Minnesota ZIPs (550–567). The transform errors early on the first
query that returns nothing useful.

**Affected derived columns (~10):** `puma`, `puma_afact`, `county`,
`county_name`, `county_afact`, `tract`, `tract_fips`, `tract_afact`,
and related allocation columns.

**Fix lives:** Data layer. Requires MN ACS extraction (also deferred)
so MN-scoped crosswalks can be built.

## 2. `caregiver relationship` — 100% NA upstream

**Symptom:** `Failed to process caregiver relationship : 'ref' must be an existing level`

**Root cause:** Fields `cqr008` (responding-caregiver type) and
`nschj013` (secondary-caregiver type) are **100% NA in live MN26
data** (10,562 of 10,562 long rows on 2026-05-11). The transform
builds `relation2` via `mapvalues(nschj013, ...)` which yields
all-NA. The subsequent `as.factor()` produces a factor with zero
levels. The `relevel(ref = "Biological or Adoptive Parent")` call
then errors because the ref label can't exist in an empty level
set.

**Affected derived columns (4):** `relation1`, `relation2`,
`female_a1`, `mom_a1`.

**Fix lives:** Upstream. Verify with NORC whether `cqr008` /
`nschj013` were renamed in the MN26 instrument and missed during
the reconciliation audit (`scripts/mn26/reconciliation_audit.R`), or
whether NORC isn't collecting them at all. If renamed → update
field-mapping config. If not collected → either accept the gap or
work with NORC to start collecting.

## 3. `education` — sparse data, root cause uncertain

**Symptom:** `Failed to process education : 'ref' must be an existing level`

**Root cause (partial):** `cqr004` (primary caregiver education) is
83% NA and `nschj017` (secondary caregiver education) is 84% NA.
The non-NA values span codes 0–5 only — no respondents at codes 6
("Bachelor's Degree"), 7 ("Master's"), or 8 ("Doctorate") in the
partial collection so far. The transform builds `educ_max` with
explicit `factor(x, levels = c("0",...,"8"), labels = ...)` which
*should* preserve all 9 levels regardless of data, but
`relevel(ref = "Bachelor's Degree (BA, BS, AB)")` still fails. The
exact level-loss mechanism wasn't isolated on 2026-05-11; debugging
needs instrumentation inside `recode__()` to print `levels(educ_max)`
right before the relevel call.

**Affected derived columns (12):** `educ_max`, `educ_a1`, `educ_a2`,
`educ_mom`, `educ4_max`, `educ4_a1`, `educ4_a2`, `educ4_mom`,
`educ6_max`, `educ6_a1`, `educ6_a2`, `educ6_mom`.

**Fix lives:** Most likely the transform code (a defensive
`relevel()` that no-ops when the factor's level-set doesn't include
the ref). The sparse-data problem may self-resolve as collection
continues and respondents at the high-education codes appear.

## When to revisit

- **Before MN26 raking work begins** — all three need to be
  functional for raking target construction.
- **If a downstream analysis needs maternal education** (e.g., SES
  descriptive tables, demographic stratification): fix `education`
  first — it's the most tractable of the three.
- **If NORC adds caregiver-2 collection** so `cqr008` / `nschj013`
  stop being 100% NA: `caregiver relationship` will start succeeding
  on its own without code change.
